---
title: "Testing, Documentation & TestPyPI"
subtitle: ": Building Quality Python Packages"
date: 05/13/2025
date-format: long
author:
  - name: Hyesop Shin
    orcid: 0000-0003-2637-7933
    email: hyesop.shin@auckland.ac.nz
    affiliations:
      - name: University of Auckland
        address: 23 Symonds Street
        city: Auckland
        postal-code: 1010
format: 
  revealjs:
      logo: Figs/uoa-logo.png
      css: logo.css
      slide-number: true
      show-slide-number: print
editor: source
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <style type="text/css">
      ul li ul li {
        font-size: 0.7em;
      }
      </style>
---

## Recap of Previous Lecture

* Building your first package (`geohello`)
* The `src/` layout
* `pyproject.toml` configuration
* Building with `uv`

## Today's Lecture

::: {.incremental}
* **<span style="color:blue">Why testing matters</span>** for geospatial code
* **<span style="color:blue">pytest</span>** fundamentals
* **<span style="color:blue">Documentation</span>** with docstrings
* **<span style="color:blue">TestPyPI</span>** publication
* **<span style="color:blue">Week 10 assessment</span>** (5% of grade)
:::

## Week 10 Deliverable

**<span style="color:red">Due: Friday 22 May, 5pm</span>**

::: {.incremental}
* **<span style="color:blue">Worth 5% of final grade</span>**
* Publish package to TestPyPI
* Test coverage >80%
* Documentation complete
* Installation verified
:::

# Part 1: Why Testing Matters

## The Silent GIS Failures

<div id="contentframe" style="position:absolute; top: 250px; left: 50px;">
<iframe src="https://giphy.com/embed/3o7TKSjRrfIPjeiVyg" width="350" height="250" frameBorder="0" class="giphy-embed" allowFullScreen></iframe>
</div>

::: {style="position:absolute; top: 250px; left: 450px; font-size: 90%;"}
**<span style="color:red">Example failures:</span>**

::: {.incremental}
* Join drops 5 zones silently
* CRS mismatch → wrong distances
* Buffer with negative value → empty geometry
* Division by zero → NaN everywhere
* **None of these raise errors!** 🚨
:::
:::

## Real Example: The Missing Zones

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
```python
# Spatial join - looks fine!
result = gpd.sjoin(boundaries, census, predicate='intersects')

print(f"Original zones: {len(boundaries)}")  # 100
print(f"After join: {len(result)}")          # 95

# WHERE DID 5 ZONES GO?! 😱
# No error raised!
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**<span style="color:blue">Without tests:</span>** Analysis continues, results are wrong

**<span style="color:blue">With tests:</span>** Caught immediately!
:::

## Real Example: The CRS Disaster

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
```python
# Calculate distance - but in wrong CRS!
gdf = gpd.read_file('sensors.gpkg')  # In EPSG:4326 (degrees!)

point1 = gdf.geometry[0]
point2 = gdf.geometry[1]

distance = point1.distance(point2)
print(f"Distance: {distance:.2f}m")  
# Output: Distance: 0.05m  <- WRONG!
# Should be: Distance: 5000m

# Map looks right, but ALL distances are wrong! 🚨
```
:::

## What Tests Catch

::: {.incremental}
* **<span style="color:blue">Silent data loss</span>** - Dropped rows, missing columns
* **<span style="color:blue">Wrong CRS</span>** - Geographic vs projected
* **<span style="color:blue">Invalid geometries</span>** - Empty, self-intersecting
* **<span style="color:blue">Incorrect calculations</span>** - Wrong formulas
* **<span style="color:blue">Breaking changes</span>** - When you update code
:::

## What Tests Give You

::: {.incremental}
* 💪 **Confidence** - Your code works
* 📖 **Documentation** - Examples of expected behaviour
* 🛡️ **Safety net** - Refactor without fear
* 🔬 **Reproducibility** - Analysis can be verified
:::

# Part 2: Testing with pytest

## Installing pytest

::: {style="font-size: 110%;"}
```bash
# Add to development dependencies
uv add --dev pytest pytest-cov

# Or manually in pyproject.toml
[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "pytest>=7.0",
    "pytest-cov>=4.0",
]
```
:::

## Your First Test

::: {style="font-size: 95%;"}
```python
# tests/test_geohello.py
from geohello import hello

def test_hello_default():
    """Test default greeting returns Auckland."""
    result = hello()
    assert "Auckland" in result
    assert "Hello from" in result

def test_hello_custom_place():
    """Test custom place name works."""
    result = hello("Tokyo")
    assert "Tokyo" in result
    assert result == "Hello from Tokyo!"

def test_hello_empty_string():
    """Test empty string defaults to Auckland."""
    result = hello("")
    assert "Auckland" in result
```
:::

## Running Tests

::: {style="font-size: 100%;"}
```bash
# Run all tests
uv run pytest

# Verbose output
uv run pytest -v

# Specific test file
uv run pytest tests/test_geohello.py

# Specific test function
uv run pytest tests/test_geohello.py::test_hello_default
```

**Output:**
```
===== test session starts =====
collected 3 items

tests/test_geohello.py ...    [100%]

===== 3 passed in 0.12s =====
```
:::

## Test Structure

::: {style="font-size: 100%;"}
```
tests/
├── __init__.py
├── test_data.py          # Test data loading
├── test_metrics.py       # Test calculations
├── test_viz.py           # Test visualizations
└── fixtures/             # Test data files
    ├── sample_sa2.gpkg
    └── sample_census.csv
```
:::

## Testing Geospatial Operations

::: {style="font-size: 80%;"}
```python
# tests/test_data.py
import pytest
import geopandas as gpd
from my_package.data import load_sa2

def test_load_sa2_returns_geodataframe():
    """Test load_sa2 returns GeoDataFrame."""
    gdf = load_sa2('tests/fixtures/sample_sa2.gpkg')
    assert isinstance(gdf, gpd.GeoDataFrame)

def test_load_sa2_has_correct_crs():
    """Test loaded data has NZTM projection."""
    gdf = load_sa2('tests/fixtures/sample_sa2.gpkg')
    assert gdf.crs == 'EPSG:2193'

def test_load_sa2_has_required_columns():
    """Test required columns present."""
    gdf = load_sa2('tests/fixtures/sample_sa2.gpkg')
    required = ['SA2_code', 'SA2_name', 'geometry']
    for col in required:
        assert col in gdf.columns

def test_load_sa2_raises_on_missing_file():
    """Test appropriate error for missing file."""
    with pytest.raises(FileNotFoundError):
        load_sa2('nonexistent.gpkg')
```
:::

## Testing CRS Operations {.smaller}

::: {style="font-size: 80%;"}
```python
# tests/test_crs.py
import geopandas as gpd
from shapely.geometry import Point
from my_package.data import ensure_nztm

def test_ensure_nztm_converts_wgs84():
    """Test conversion from WGS84 to NZTM."""
    # Auckland Sky Tower in WGS84
    gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
        {'name': ['Sky Tower']},
        geometry=[Point(174.7633, -36.8485)],
        crs='EPSG:4326'
    )
    
    result = ensure_nztm(gdf)
    
    assert result.crs == 'EPSG:2193'
    # Check coordinates are in expected range (NZTM)
    x, y = result.geometry[0].x, result.geometry[0].y
    assert 1_700_000 < x < 1_800_000  # NZTM easting
    assert 5_900_000 < y < 6_000_000  # NZTM northing

def test_ensure_nztm_preserves_nztm():
    """Test NZTM data unchanged."""
    gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
        {'name': ['Test']},
        geometry=[Point(1750000, 5920000)],
        crs='EPSG:2193'
    )
    
    result = ensure_nztm(gdf)
    
    assert result.crs == 'EPSG:2193'
    assert result.geometry[0].x == 1750000
```
:::

## Testing Spatial Calculations

::: {style="font-size: 75%;"}
```python
# tests/test_metrics.py
import geopandas as gpd
import pytest
from shapely.geometry import Polygon
from my_package.metrics import calculate_density

def test_calculate_density():
    """Test density calculation."""
    # Create test polygon (1000m x 1000m = 1 km²)
    poly = Polygon([
        (0, 0), (1000, 0), (1000, 1000), (0, 1000), (0, 0)
    ])
    
    gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
        {'population': [2000]},
        geometry=[poly],
        crs='EPSG:2193'  # NZTM (metres)
    )
    
    result = calculate_density(gdf)
    
    assert 'density' in result.columns
    # 2000 people / 1 km² = 2000
    assert result['density'].iloc[0] == pytest.approx(2000, rel=0.01)

def test_calculate_density_zero_area():
    """Test handling of zero-area geometries."""
    line = Polygon([(0, 0), (1000, 0), (1000, 0), (0, 0)])  # Degenerate
    
    gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
        {'population': [100]},
        geometry=[line],
        crs='EPSG:2193'
    )
    
    result = calculate_density(gdf)
    
    # Should handle gracefully
    assert result['density'].iloc[0] in [0, float('nan')]
```
:::

## pytest Features

**<span style="color:blue">1. Parametrize</span>** - Test multiple cases

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
```python
@pytest.mark.parametrize("crs_from,crs_to", [
    ('EPSG:4326', 'EPSG:2193'),
    ('EPSG:4326', 'EPSG:3857'),
    ('EPSG:2193', 'EPSG:4326'),
])
def test_reproject_multiple_crs(crs_from, crs_to):
    """Test reprojection between different CRS pairs."""
    gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
        {'id': [1]},
        geometry=[Point(174.76, -36.85)],
        crs=crs_from
    )
    
    result = gdf.to_crs(crs_to)
    assert result.crs == crs_to
```
:::

---

### pytest Features (continued)

**<span style="color:blue">2. Fixtures</span>** - Reusable test data

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
```python
@pytest.fixture
def sample_sa2():
    """Fixture providing sample SA2 data."""
    return gpd.GeoDataFrame({
        'SA2_code': ['001', '002', '003'],
        'SA2_name': ['CBD', 'Newmarket', 'Ponsonby'],
        'geometry': [
            Polygon([(0, 0), (10, 0), (10, 10), (0, 10)]),
            Polygon([(10, 0), (20, 0), (20, 10), (10, 10)]),
            Polygon([(0, 10), (10, 10), (10, 20), (0, 20)]),
        ]
    }, crs='EPSG:2193')

def test_with_fixture(sample_sa2):
    """Test using fixture data."""
    assert len(sample_sa2) == 3
    assert sample_sa2.crs == 'EPSG:2193'
```
:::

## Your Turn: Write Tests

::: {style="font-size: 110%;"}
**<span style="color:blue">Task</span>**: Write tests for your `geohello` package

1. Create `tests/test_geohello.py`
2. Write 3-5 test functions
3. Run with `uv run pytest`
4. Verify all pass

**Time**: 15 minutes
:::

## Test Coverage

::: {style="font-size: 100%;"}
```bash
# Run tests with coverage
uv run pytest --cov=geohello --cov-report=term-missing

# Output shows what's covered
```

```
----------- coverage -----------
Name                    Stmts   Miss  Cover   Missing
-----------------------------------------------------
geohello/__init__.py        5      0   100%
geohello/core.py           23      2    91%   45-46
-----------------------------------------------------
TOTAL                      28      2    93%
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**<span style="color:blue">Target for Week 10</span>**: >80% coverage
:::

# Part 3: Documentation

## Why Document?

<div id="contentframe" style="position:absolute; top: 250px; left: 50px;">
<iframe src="https://giphy.com/embed/l0IyjiXOXTX6Yemsg" width="350" height="250" frameBorder="0" class="giphy-embed" allowFullScreen></iframe>
</div>

::: {style="position:absolute; top: 280px; left: 450px;"}
**<span style="color:blue">You in 6 months:</span>**

"What does this function do?!"

**<span style="color:blue">Your colleague:</span>**

"How do I use this?"

**<span style="color:blue">Documentation saves everyone!</span>**
:::

## NumPy Style Docstrings

::: {style="font-size: 75%;"}
```python
def calculate_accessibility(
    origins_gdf,
    destinations_gdf,
    max_distance_m=800
):
    """
    Calculate accessibility scores based on nearby destinations.
    
    Counts the number of destinations within walking distance of each origin.
    Assumes planar coordinates in metres.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    origins_gdf : GeoDataFrame
        Points representing origin locations (e.g., homes)
    destinations_gdf : GeoDataFrame
        Points representing destinations (e.g., shops, parks)
    max_distance_m : float, default 800
        Maximum walking distance in metres
    
    Returns
    -------
    GeoDataFrame
        Origins with added 'accessibility_score' column containing
        count of nearby destinations
    
    Raises
    ------
    ValueError
        If CRS is not projected
    
    Examples
    --------
    >>> homes = gpd.read_file('homes.gpkg')
    >>> shops = gpd.read_file('shops.gpkg')
    >>> result = calculate_accessibility(homes, shops, max_distance_m=400)
    >>> result['accessibility_score'].mean()
    3.2
    
    Notes
    -----
    Uses Euclidean distance. For network-based accessibility, use
    r5py routing instead.
    """
    # Implementation here
    pass
```
:::

## Docstring Sections

::: {.incremental}
* **<span style="color:blue">Short summary</span>** - One line description
* **<span style="color:blue">Parameters</span>** - Inputs with types
* **<span style="color:blue">Returns</span>** - What it gives back
* **<span style="color:blue">Raises</span>** - What errors it might throw
* **<span style="color:blue">Examples</span>** - How to use it
* **<span style="color:blue">Notes</span>** - Additional info
:::

## README Documentation

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
````markdown
# geohello

A simple geospatial greeting package.

## Installation

```bash
pip install geohello-yourname
```

## Quick Start

```python
from geohello import hello

print(hello())
# Output: Hello from Auckland!

print(hello("Tokyo"))
# Output: Hello from Tokyo!
```

## Features

- Simple greeting function
- Customizable location
- Well-tested and documented

## Development

```bash
# Install with dev dependencies
uv pip install -e ".[dev]"

# Run tests
uv run pytest
```

## License

MIT License
````
:::

# Part 4: Publishing to TestPyPI

## What is TestPyPI?

::: {.incremental}
* **<span style="color:blue">Test version</span>** of Python Package Index
* **<span style="color:blue">Practice</span>** publication workflow
* **<span style="color:blue">Safe to experiment</span>** - no consequences
* **<span style="color:blue">Packages deleted after 6 months</span>**
* **<span style="color:blue">Perfect for learning!</span>**
:::

## TestPyPI vs PyPI

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
| Feature | TestPyPI | Production PyPI |
|---------|----------|-----------------|
| **Purpose** | Testing | Production |
| **URL** | test.pypi.org | pypi.org |
| **Permanence** | 6 months | Forever |
| **Dependencies** | Limited | Full |
| **Mistakes** | Don't matter | Very visible |
:::

## Step 1: Create Account

::: {.incremental}
1. Visit **<span style="color:blue">https://test.pypi.org/account/register/</span>**
2. Verify email
3. Enable 2FA (recommended)
4. Generate API token
:::

![](https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/_images/token-generation.png){fig-align=center width=50%}

## Step 2: Generate API Token

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
**In TestPyPI:**

1. Account Settings → API Tokens
2. Click "Add API token"
3. Name: "uv-publish-geohello"
4. Scope: "Entire account"
5. Click "Create token"
6. **<span style="color:red">Copy token immediately!</span>** (starts with `pypi-`)
7. Save securely

**⚠️ You can't view it again!**
:::

## Step 3: Build Package

::: {style="font-size: 100%;"}
```bash
# Check version in pyproject.toml
[project]
version = "0.1.0"  # Must be unique!

# Build
uv build

# Verify dist/ contains both files
ls dist/
```

**Output:**
```
geohello_yourname-0.1.0-py3-none-any.whl
geohello_yourname-0.1.0.tar.gz
```
:::

## Step 4: Publish to TestPyPI

::: {style="font-size: 95%;"}
```bash
# Publish with token
uv publish --index testpypi --token pypi-YOUR_TOKEN_HERE

# Or set environment variable
export UV_PUBLISH_TOKEN=pypi-YOUR_TOKEN_HERE
uv publish --index testpypi
```

**Success:**
```
Uploading distributions to https://test.pypi.org/legacy/
Uploading geohello_yourname-0.1.0-py3-none-any.whl
Uploading geohello_yourname-0.1.0.tar.gz
✓ Successfully published

View at: https://test.pypi.org/project/geohello-yourname/
```
:::

## Step 5: Verify Installation

::: {style="font-size: 95%;"}
```bash
# Create fresh environment
uv venv test-install
source test-install/bin/activate

# Install from TestPyPI
pip install --index-url https://test.pypi.org/simple/ \
    geohello-yourname

# Test import
python -c "from geohello import hello; print(hello())"

# Should print: Hello from Auckland!
```
:::

## Common Errors

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**Error 1: Name already taken**
```
HTTPError: 403 Forbidden
The name 'geohello' is already taken
```
**Solution**: Add your name: `geohello-yourname`

**Error 2: Version already published**
```
HTTPError: 400 Bad Request
File already exists
```
**Solution**: Increment version: `0.1.0` → `0.1.1`

**Error 3: Invalid token**
```
HTTPError: 403 Forbidden
```
**Solution**: Generate new token, check scope
:::

## Your Turn: Publish to TestPyPI

::: {style="font-size: 110%;"}
**<span style="color:blue">Task</span>**: Publish your package

1. Create TestPyPI account
2. Generate API token
3. Build: `uv build`
4. Publish: `uv publish --index testpypi --token TOKEN`
5. Verify installation
6. Screenshot for submission

**Time**: 30 minutes
:::

# Week 10 Deliverable

## What to Submit (5% of Grade)

**<span style="color:red">Due: Friday 22 May, 5pm</span>**

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
**1. TestPyPI URL**
- Link to your published package
- Example: `https://test.pypi.org/project/geohello-yourname/`

**2. GitHub Repository**
- Complete package code
- Tests with >80% coverage
- Comprehensive README

**3. Installation Screenshot**
- Shows successful install from TestPyPI
- Working import
- Function execution

**4. Brief Reflection** (150-200 words)
- What challenges did you encounter?
- How did you resolve them?
:::

## Evaluation Criteria

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
| Criterion | Points | What we're looking for |
|-----------|--------|------------------------|
| **Published** | 2% | Visible on TestPyPI, downloadable |
| **Tests** | 1% | ≥3 tests, >80% coverage |
| **Documentation** | 1% | Docstrings + README |
| **Installation** | 1% | Works in fresh environment |

**Total: 5% of final grade**
:::

## Assignment 3 Preview

**<span style="color:blue">Week 12 (30% of grade):</span>**

::: {.incremental}
* Publish to **real PyPI**
* More comprehensive functionality
* Higher test coverage
* Professional documentation
* Optional: CI/CD pipeline
:::

## Tips for Success

::: {.incremental}
* **<span style="color:blue">Start early</span>** - Don't wait until Friday
* **<span style="color:blue">Test everything</span>** - Coverage > 80%
* **<span style="color:blue">Document well</span>** - Future you will thank you
* **<span style="color:blue">Ask for help</span>** - Office hours, Ed discussion
* **<span style="color:blue">Have fun!</span>** - You're publishing real software!
:::

## Summary

::: {.incremental}
* **Testing** prevents silent failures
* **pytest** makes testing easy
* **Docstrings** document your code
* **TestPyPI** is safe practice
* **Week 10 deliverable** worth 5%
:::

## Next Week

**<span style="color:blue">Week 11: Production Quality Packages</span>**

::: {.incremental}
* Complete package examples
* CI/CD with GitHub Actions
* Publishing to real PyPI
* Poster design for showcase
:::

## Resources

* pytest documentation: docs.pytest.org
* NumPy docstring guide: numpydoc.readthedocs.io
* TestPyPI: test.pypi.org
* uv publish guide: docs.astral.sh/uv/guides/publish

## Thanks! <br> Q & A {style="text-align: center;"}

**Week 10 deliverable due Friday!**

<div style="width:840px"><iframe allow="fullscreen" frameBorder="0" height="480" src="https://giphy.com/embed/3o6Zt6ML6BklcajjsA/video" width="840"></iframe></div>
